title: Average atomic mass
definition: An element's atoms come in isotopes — same proton count, different neutron counts, so different masses. The standard atomic weight on the periodic table is the **abundance-weighted average** of those isotope masses, $\bar{A} = \sum_i f_i A_i$, where each $f_i$ is an isotope's fractional abundance and $A_i$ its mass. The averaging arithmetic is exact; the abundances and isotope masses are sourced measurements (CIAAW). That single averaged number is the atomic weight every molar mass in this Atlas sums.
